metadata.description: Policy continuity chains — how key Indian policies have evolved across governments
metadata.note: Each chain tracks a policy theme through successive governments, showing renaming, restructuring, and evolution

timeline.name: National Food for Work Programme
timeline.description: Food grain-based wage employment in 150 most backward districts

timeline.name: NREGA (National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)
timeline.description: Landmark legislation guaranteeing 100 days of wage employment per rural household. Started in 200 districts.

timeline.name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi NREGA)
timeline.description: Renamed and expanded to all rural districts. Remains India's largest work guarantee programme with ~26 crore active workers.

chains.analysis: Despite political rhetoric about replacing MGNREGA, the programme has survived across NDA I, II, and III — though budget allocations relative to GDP have fluctuated. The NDA governments have reframed it as a rural asset creation programme rather than a welfare scheme.

timeline.name: Central Rural Sanitation Programme (CRSP)
timeline.description: India's first national sanitation programme — supply-driven, focused on toilet construction

timeline.name: Total Sanitation Campaign (TSC)
timeline.description: Shifted to demand-driven approach with IEC, school sanitation, and Nirmal Gram Puraskar incentive

timeline.name: Nirmal Bharat Abhiyan (NBA)
timeline.description: Restructured TSC with higher subsidies, focus on community-led total sanitation, and saturation approach

timeline.name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Gramin (SBM-G)
timeline.description: Complete rebranding with massive political capital. Target: ODF India by Oct 2019. Budget increased 5x. Declared India ODF on 2 Oct 2019.

timeline.name: SBM-G Phase II
timeline.description: Focus shifted from construction to sustainability — ODF-Plus, solid/liquid waste management, GOBARdhan for biogas. Rs 1.4 lakh crore outlay.

chains.analysis: 40-year policy continuity despite 6 renamings. Each government built on predecessors' infrastructure while adding their political branding. The fundamental shift was SBM's political prioritisation — the policy itself was iterative, but the implementation intensity was unprecedented.

timeline.name: Unique Identification Authority (UIDAI) / Aadhaar
timeline.description: Nandan Nilekani-led biometric identity project. Initially voluntary, no statutory backing.

timeline.name: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)
timeline.description: Aadhaar-linked direct cash transfer to bank accounts, replacing subsidy intermediaries

timeline.name: JAM Trinity (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile)
timeline.description: NDA repackaged UPA's Aadhaar+DBT with massive Jan Dhan financial inclusion drive. Rs 3.3 lakh crore transferred via DBT by 2025.

timeline.name: Aadhaar Act, 2016
timeline.description: Gave statutory backing to Aadhaar (passed as Money Bill to bypass Rajya Sabha). Supreme Court upheld with restrictions in 2018.

timeline.name: India Stack / DigiLocker / UPI Expansion
timeline.description: Full digital public infrastructure stack — UPI handles 14 billion transactions/month. Aadhaar crosses 1.3 billion enrollments.

chains.analysis: Rare case of genuine cross-party policy continuity. UPA conceived Aadhaar; NDA expanded and gave it legal backing. Both sides claim credit. The underlying DPI approach has become India's most significant governance export (adopted by 10+ countries).

timeline.name: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA)
timeline.description: Universalisation of elementary education. Built infrastructure — 3.5 lakh schools, 20 lakh teachers added.

timeline.name: Right to Education Act (RTE)
timeline.description: Made education a fundamental right (6-14 years). 25% reservation in private schools for EWS. Landmark legislation.

timeline.name: Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA)
timeline.description: Extended SSA approach to secondary education — universal access to secondary level by 2017

timeline.name: Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan
timeline.description: Merged SSA, RMSA, and Teacher Education into single integrated scheme covering pre-school to Class XII

timeline.name: National Education Policy 2020 (NEP)
timeline.description: Replaced 34-year-old NEP 1986. 5+3+3+4 structure, mother tongue instruction, multidisciplinary universities, 6% GDP target. Most ambitious education overhaul since independence.

chains.analysis: Education policy shows strongest continuity — each government builds on existing infrastructure while adding new layers. SSA's school-building was necessary before RTE could mandate access. NEP 2020 is genuinely transformative in scope but implementation is uneven across states.

timeline.name: Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY)
timeline.description: First national health insurance for BPL families. Rs 30,000 coverage for family of 5. Covered 3.6 crore families.

timeline.name: Ayushman Bharat - PM-JAY
timeline.description: World's largest public health insurance. Rs 5 lakh/family/year for 55 crore beneficiaries. Subsumed RSBY. 6.2 crore hospital admissions by 2025.

timeline.name: Ayushman Bharat - Health & Wellness Centres
timeline.description: 1.5 lakh sub-centres upgraded to deliver comprehensive primary healthcare — preventive + promotive care at doorstep

timeline.name: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)
timeline.description: Digital health ID (ABHA), health records integration, telemedicine infrastructure. 55 crore ABHA IDs created.

chains.analysis: RSBY was a modest pilot; PM-JAY scaled it 15x in coverage amount and 15x in beneficiaries. The NDA approach was to build an entire health ecosystem (insurance + primary care + digital) rather than standalone insurance. State-level schemes (e.g., Tamil Nadu's CMCHIS, Kerala's KASP) run in parallel.

timeline.name: Indira Awaas Yojana (IAY)
timeline.description: Rural housing for BPL families. Built ~3.5 crore houses over 20 years. Unit cost Rs 70,000-75,000.

timeline.name: Rajiv Awaas Yojana (RAY)
timeline.description: Slum-free India vision — in-situ rehabilitation, affordable housing, property rights for slum dwellers

timeline.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
timeline.description: Replaced IAY. Unit cost doubled to Rs 1.2-1.3 lakh. Pucca house with toilet, LPG, electricity, water. 2.95 crore houses sanctioned.

timeline.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
timeline.description: Replaced RAY. 'Housing for All by 2022' mission. 1.18 crore houses sanctioned. Credit-Linked Subsidy Scheme for middle-income.

chains.analysis: Clear policy continuity with significant scale-up. IAY built 3.5 crore houses in 20 years; PMAY-G targeted 2.95 crore in 6 years with 2x unit cost. The NDA innovation was converging multiple services (toilet, gas, water, electricity) into the housing unit — not just walls and roof.

timeline.name: LPG Subsidy (historical)
timeline.description: Universal LPG subsidy — benefited primarily urban middle class. Rs 50,000+ crore annual subsidy bill by 2014.

timeline.name: PAHAL (Direct Benefit Transfer of LPG Subsidy)
timeline.description: Aadhaar-linked direct transfer replaced physical subsidy. 'GiveItUp' campaign — 1.13 crore consumers voluntarily surrendered subsidy.

timeline.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
timeline.description: Free LPG connections to BPL women. 10.35 crore connections by 2024. Transformed rural cooking from biomass to LPG.

timeline.name: PMUY 2.0
timeline.description: Extended to migrant families. First refill and stove free. Target: additional 1 crore connections. But refill rates remain a concern — many households revert to biomass.

chains.analysis: Ujjwala is one of the most successful access programmes — 10 crore connections is transformative. But the sustainability challenge is real: refill purchase rates suggest 25-30% of beneficiaries can't afford regular LPG use, reverting to biomass. The policy solved access but not affordability.

timeline.name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY)
timeline.description: All-weather road connectivity to unconnected habitations (500+ population in plains, 250+ in hills). 7.25 lakh km built.

timeline.name: PMGSY-II
timeline.description: Upgrade of existing through-routes and major rural links. Consolidated earlier road network improvements.

timeline.name: PMGSY-III
timeline.description: Connecting habitations to schools, hospitals, markets via 1.25 lakh km of road consolidation routes

chains.analysis: Model of unbroken policy continuity across 4 governments and 25+ years. Vajpayee initiated, UPA expanded, NDA II & III sustained. PMGSY is arguably India's most successful infrastructure programme — 98% of eligible habitations now connected. Zero political controversy.

timeline.name: Rythu Bandhu (Telangana state scheme)
timeline.description: First direct income transfer to farmers — Rs 10,000/acre/year. Pioneered the unconditional farm income model in India.

timeline.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
timeline.description: Rs 6,000/year in 3 instalments to all landholding farmers. 9 crore beneficiaries. Rs 3.04 lakh crore distributed by 2025.

timeline.name: PM-KISAN Maan Dhan (Pension)
timeline.description: Pension scheme for small/marginal farmers — Rs 3,000/month after age 60. Contributory scheme co-paid by government.

chains.analysis: Interesting case of state-to-centre policy diffusion. Telangana's Rythu Bandhu (2018) directly inspired PM-KISAN (2019). PM-KISAN's pre-election timing was politically significant. The shift from price support (MSP) to income support represents a fundamental policy pivot.

timeline.name: Swabhimaan / Financial Inclusion Plan
timeline.description: Banking access through Business Correspondents in 74,000 villages. Opened 7 crore basic savings accounts.

timeline.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
timeline.description: Universal bank account scheme — zero-balance accounts with RuPay debit card, Rs 2 lakh accident insurance. 53 crore accounts opened. World record for financial inclusion.

timeline.name: PM Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
timeline.description: Micro-enterprise loans up to Rs 10 lakh without collateral. 47 crore loans worth Rs 27.75 lakh crore sanctioned. Three tiers: Shishu, Kishore, Tarun.

timeline.name: PM SVANidhi (Street Vendor Scheme)
timeline.description: Working capital loans for street vendors — Rs 10,000 to Rs 50,000. 78 lakh loans sanctioned. Digital payments incentives.

chains.analysis: UPA's Swabhimaan opened 7 crore accounts; NDA's Jan Dhan opened 53 crore — the scale difference was decisive. PMJDY became the infrastructure layer for all subsequent DBT, PM-KISAN, and COVID relief transfers. Financial inclusion was the precondition for India's digital welfare state.

timeline.name: Jawaharlal Nehru National Solar Mission (JNNSM)
timeline.description: Target: 20 GW solar by 2022. Three phases. India's first serious solar push — but target was modest.

timeline.name: Revised Solar Target: 100 GW by 2022
timeline.description: PM Modi's Paris Climate pledge. 5x increase in solar target. International Solar Alliance co-founded with France.

timeline.name: 500 GW Non-Fossil Fuel by 2030
timeline.description: COP26 Glasgow pledge. Includes 280 GW solar, 140 GW wind. Green Hydrogen Mission (Rs 19,744 crore). PM-KUSUM for farmers.

timeline.name: Net Zero 2070 & Green Energy Corridors
timeline.description: Long-term decarbonisation strategy. Green Energy Corridor Phase II for renewable evacuation infrastructure. Carbon market initiated.

chains.analysis: Solar policy shows exponential ambition scaling: 20 GW (UPA) → 100 GW (NDA I) → 500 GW non-fossil (NDA II). Actual capacity reached ~200 GW by 2025. The policy continuity is strong but execution lags targets consistently. India's green energy story is genuine but aspirational timelines are unrealistic.

timeline.name: National Rural Drinking Water Programme (NRDWP)
timeline.description: Restructured Accelerated Rural Water Supply Programme. Target: 55 LPCD supply. Coverage was measured as 'habitations covered' — misleading metric.

timeline.name: Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)
timeline.description: Functional Household Tap Connection (FHTC) to every rural household by 2024. Rs 3.6 lakh crore outlay. Shifted metric from 'habitation' to 'household' — far more rigorous. 14.5 crore connections (75%+ coverage) by 2025.

chains.analysis: JJM represents a genuine policy discontinuity — not just renaming. The shift from 'habitation-level' to 'household-level' tap water was a fundamentally different target. Scale and speed are unprecedented. But sustainability (water source, O&M funding, water quality) remains the challenge.
